prompt:
Context: our alerting service lets on-call folks attach little threshold formulas to alert rules. We evaluate them as RPN (postfix) so there's no parser to maintain — but the current evaluator is a Python eval() bridge and it has to die this sprint. Ticket: implement the stack machine in Rust, in this crate (rs-rpnvm), as src/lib.rs. The acceptance tests in tests/vm.rs are already written and reviewed; don't modify tests/, Cargo.toml, or .gitignore. Stdlib only.

Example session so you can see the shape: vm.eval("3 4 + 2 *") => Ok(14). vm.set_var("rate", 250); vm.eval("rate 4 *") => Ok(1000). vm.eval("5 !x x x +") => Ok(10) and afterwards get_var("x") == Some(5).

API: pub struct Vm with Vm::new(max_depth: usize), set_var(&mut self, name: &str, value: i64), get_var(&self, name: &str) -> Option<i64>, eval(&mut self, program: &str) -> Result<i64, VmError>. Variables persist across eval calls on the same Vm; the value stack does NOT — every eval starts empty. A program must finish with exactly one value on the stack; that's the result.

Tokens (program split on whitespace; token indices are 0-based over that list):
- integer literal: optional leading '-', then ASCII digits, i64 range. Something that starts like a number but isn't one ("12x", out-of-range like "9223372036854775808") is BadLiteral. Note i64::MIN is a legal literal.
- binary ops + - * / %: pop right operand, then left, push (left op right). Division/remainder follow Rust integer semantics: / truncates toward zero, % takes the dividend's sign. All arithmetic is CHECKED — wraps are bugs, so i64 overflow (including i64::MIN / -1, i64::MIN % -1, i64::MIN neg) is ArithmeticOverflow; dividing or remaindering by zero is DivideByZero.
- neg: pop, push checked negation.
- dup, swap, drop: the usual stack ops (dup pushes a copy, swap exchanges the top two, drop discards the top).
- identifier [a-z_][a-z0-9_]*: push that variable's value; unknown variable is UndefinedVar.
- !identifier (e.g. !x): pop the top and store it into the variable. A '!' not followed by a valid identifier is an UnknownToken.
- anything else (uppercase, symbols, '--5'): UnknownToken.

Guards: the stack has a hard capacity (max_depth from the constructor). Any push that would exceed it — including literals, variable loads, and dup — fails with StackOverflow carrying the token index and the depth at refusal (== the configured limit). Ops that need more operands than are present fail with StackUnderflow carrying the token index and the token text.

pub enum VmError (derive Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq), exact variants: UnknownToken { index: usize, token: String }, BadLiteral { index: usize, token: String }, UndefinedVar { index: usize, name: String }, StackUnderflow { index: usize, token: String }, StackOverflow { index: usize, depth: usize }, DivideByZero { index: usize }, ArithmeticOverflow { index: usize }, BadResult { depth: usize } (program ended with depth != 1). Implement Display + std::error::Error; the tests pin a few exact messages, e.g. UnknownToken renders as: token 2: unknown token "&" — and BadResult as: program left 2 values on the stack (expected exactly 1).

Semantics detail the tests rely on: evaluation is a single left-to-right pass, first error aborts, and side effects already performed stick (a !store before a bad token persists in the vars map). Use a BTreeMap for vars. Acceptance: cargo test --offline green.
